content.text: Now implementing the change with a separate 3-field tuple class for the `with_counts=True` case, keeping backward compatibility for the default case.

content.content: The file /workspace/repo/src/jinja2/filters.py has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +    # Use the regular tuple repr to hide this subclass if users print
"""Group a sequence of objects by an attribute using Python's
:func:`itertools.groupby`. The attribute can use dot notation for
+    You can pass ``with_counts=True`` to also get the size of each group
+    without having to count the items yourself, yielding
+        <ul>{% for city, items, count in users|groupby("city", with_counts=True) %}
The attribute supports dot notation for nested access.
+        for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr):
for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr)
+        for key, values in groupby(sorted_value, expr):
-        for key, values in groupby(sorted(await auto_to_list(value), key=expr), expr)
+        for key, values in groupby(sorted_value, expr)

input.command:
python -c "
from jinja2 import Environment
env = Environment()
tmpl = env.from_string('''{% for city, items, count in users|groupby(\"city\", with_counts=True) %}{{ city }}:{{ count }} {% endfor %}''')
users = [{'city':'NY','name':'a'},{'city':'NY','name':'b'},{'city':'LA','name':'c'}]
print(tmpl.render(users=users))

tmpl2 = env.from_string('''{% for city, items in users|groupby(\"city\") %}{{ city }}:{{ items|length }} {% endfor %}''')
print(tmpl2.render(users=users))
"

content.text: Both work. Let's also test the async variant and run the existing test suite for filters.
All tests pass. Now let's test the async path quickly.

input.command:
python -c "
import asyncio
from jinja2 import Environment
env = Environment(enable_async=True)
tmpl = env.from_string('''{% for city, items, count in users|groupby(\"city\", with_counts=True) %}{{ city }}:{{ count }} {% endfor %}''')
users = [{'city':'NY','name':'a'},{'city':'NY','name':'b'},{'city':'LA','name':'c'}]
print(asyncio.run(tmpl.render_async(users=users)))
"

content.text: Now let's write the final diff.

The attribute can use dot notation for nested access, like ``"address.city"``. Unlike Python's ``groupby``, @@ -1203,6 +1218,19 @@ def sync_do_groupby(
  • {{ city }}: {{ items|map(attribute="name")|join(", ") }}
    • {% endfor %} + You can pass ``with_counts=True`` to also get the size of each group + without having to count the items yourself, yielding + ``(grouper, list, count)`` tuples instead. + + .. sourcecode:: jinja + +
      {% for city, items, count in users|groupby("city", with_counts=True) %} +
    • {{ city }} ({{ count }})
      • + {% endfor %}
    + + .. versionchanged:: 3.2 + Added the ``with_counts`` parameter. + .. versionchanged:: 3.0 Added the ``default`` parameter. @@ -1210,6 +1238,16 @@ def sync_do_groupby( The attribute supports dot notation for nested access. """ expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) + + if with_counts: + groups = [] + + for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr): + items = list(values) + groups.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, items, len(items))) + + return groups + return [ _GroupTuple(key, list(values)) for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr) @@ -1222,11 +1260,23 @@ async def do_groupby( value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]", attribute: t.Union[str, int], default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, -) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": + with_counts: bool = False, +) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, ...]]": expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) + sorted_value = sorted(await auto_to_list(value), key=expr) + + if with_counts: + groups = [] + + for key, values in groupby(sorted_value, expr): + items = await auto_to_list(values) + groups.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, items, len(items))) + + return groups + return [ _GroupTuple(key, await auto_to_list(values)) - for key, values in groupby(sorted(await auto_to_list(value), key=expr), expr) + for key, values in groupby(sorted_value, expr) ]

content.text: Implemented the `with_counts` parameter for the `groupby` filter in `src/jinja2/filters.py`. When `with_counts=True`, both `sync_do_groupby` and the async `do_groupby` yield `(grouper, list, count)` tuples via a new `_GroupTupleWithCount` namedtuple; the default `False` path is unchanged, preserving backward compatibility with 2-tuple unpacking. Full test suite (837 tests) passes, and I manually verified sync/async rendering. Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.

result: Implemented the `with_counts` parameter for the `groupby` filter in `src/jinja2/filters.py`. When `with_counts=True`, both `sync_do_groupby` and the async `do_groupby` yield `(grouper, list, count)` tuples via a new `_GroupTupleWithCount` namedtuple; the default `False` path is unchanged, preserving backward compatibility with 2-tuple unpacking. Full test suite (837 tests) passes, and I manually verified sync/async rendering. Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.
